title: Third place for Jonga High in provincial eisteddfod
description: GEORGE NEWS - The septet (seven voices) of...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The septet (seven voices) of Jonga High School has achieved a third position in their category at the Western Cape ABC Motsepe South Africa Schools Choral Eisteddfod (SASCE), which took place in Cape Town last weekend.


> In addition to the septet, the Thembalethu school participated in two categories, Western piece and solo tenor, getting a fourth place in both.


The school failed to defend its overall 2023 championship title.

Winners in the 2024 Motsepe SASCE Western Cape Eisteddfod

Best male conductor: Mr M Mdingi (Fezeka High)

Best female conductor: N Machona (Chris Hani High)

Soprano solo (Mein Herr Marquise):

1. Makapula High (Gcina Mkontwana)

2. Fezeka High (Siphokazi Diamond)

3. New Eisleben High (Thulisile Manciya)

Bass solo: SBP Mnomiya (Yimi lo!)

The Jonga High Septet (seven voices) achieved a third place. Back from left are Khalipha Macanda, Asemahle Noni, Mahlubodwa Zono (conductor), Asiyolise Keli and Lusanele Mhlane. Front: Khakhalethu Kedama, Owam Zantsi and Siphokuhle Mathandela.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
